title: [cross-impl] Trickle max_interval saturation Python vs Rust/Zephyr
description:
The recent fix in python/src/lichen/rpl/trickle.py:70 (commit 68e8b8209) aligned the half/range bias-free transmit_time calc to match Rust (lichen-rpl/src/trickle.rs:126) and C (lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/trickle.c:43). However, max_interval computation still differs:

- Python: self.max_interval = imin_ms << imax_doublings (arbitrary precision, no cap)
- Rust: checked_shl+checked_mul .unwrap_or(u32::MAX)
- C: explicit check for imax_doublings>=32 or overflow → UINT32_MAX

This can cause different doubling behavior for imax_doublings>=30 on typical Imin. See Rust tests for 31/32 doublings and C saturation logic. Same algorithm, different behavior on edge cases. Test vectors / interop affected.

References: project-LICHEN-verh, project-LICHEN-67ca, RFC 6206 §4.2.
